sent_en: I don't give a damn what you do and what you say.

sent_en: Put it in piles and take a pitchfork and put it in the wagon.

sent_lc: To manj avek ìn fourshèt é pa avek tô dwa-yé.
sent_en: You eat with a fork and not with your fingers.

sent_lc: To foutimen kouyon twa.
sent_en: You're really a jerk.

sent_lc: Li t'apé babiyé avek mô frèr.
sent_en: He was fussing with my brother.

sent_lc: Si tô manjé tro sho, to gìn pou fredi li, pou to koné manjé li.
sent_en: If your food is too hot, you have to let it cool, so you can eat it.

sent_lc: Ina ìn bon frèsh a larivyær.
sent_en: There was a coole breeze off the river.

sent_lc: Ìn bon ver dolo fret, çé bon kan yé fé sho.
sent_en: A good glass of cold water, that's good when it's hot.

sent_lc: Mo fri pa dézwit. Mo lìnm pa dézwit fri. Mo manj pa ça.
sent_en: I don't fry oysters. I don't like fried oysters. I don't eat that.

sent_lc:
1. Fwa dìr çé bon dan frikasé koshon.
2. O vou kwi kirey konm nimprt ki, vou ka fé ìn sauce o frikasé.
sent_en:
1. Pig's liver is good in pork fricassee.
2. Oh youcook squirrel like anything else, you can make a sauce of fricassee it.

sent_lc: To fé fromaj avek lakrem dìlé lavash.
sent_en: You make cheese with the cream of cow's milk.

sent_lc: Mo manj ìn ta frwi. Mo lìnm ìn morso banan, ìn morso depom.
sent_en: I eat a lot of fruit. I like a piece of banana, a piece of apple.

sent_lc: Trwa fwa par jou.
sent_en: Three times a day.

sent_lc:
1. Ça çé mô bag li don mòkan no fyansé.
2. Ça çé mô fyansé. M'ap alé maryé li. Dan Janviyé, li sré mo madam.
sent_en:
1. That's my ring he gave me when we got engaged.
2. That's my fiancé. I'm going to marry her. In January, she'll be my wife.

sent_lc: Li t'apé marshé kom ìn pon. Li té si fyær.
sent_en: He was walking like a peacock. He was so proud.

meaning: jumble, mess, mixture of all sorts of things
sent_lc: Mo met si-telman ìn ta diferan jardinaj endan mô frikasé ça tournen ìn galimacha.
sent_en: I put so many different vegetables into my fricassee that it turned into a hodge-podge.

meaning: to chatter, to run off at the mouth

1. to run
2. to function (to work, to run correctly)
3. to flow (like water)
4. to run into (galopé endan)
5. to date (to run around with: galopé avek)
6. to run after (chase)
7. to run a store, business, etc (to manage, operate)
8. to run a fever
sent_lc:
1. To peu pa galopé avek mwa, Torti, parské mo galop tro vit.
2. Mô lapandil ap galopé byin. Li per jamen lær.
3. Dolo-la apé galopé vit vit vit. Kouran-la fo.
4. Mo galopé endan la tab é mo fé mô mal.
5. To galopé avek ìn femn ina tou kalité nonm ki apé galopé avek li.
6. Li té gen dé shyìn, chyìn-yé yé té galopé lapen.
7. Mo galopé laletri sez an.
8. Yé sra bavé ìn ta, galopé kom ìn piti for fyev.
sent_en:
1. You can't run with me Turtle, because I run too fast.
2. My  clock is working well. It never loses time.
3. The water is flowing really fast. The current is strong.
4. I ran into the table and I hurt myself.
5. You go out with a woman, all kinds of men are going out with her.
6. He had two dogs, the dogs chased rabbits.
7. I ran the diary for sixteen years.
8. They would drool a lot and run like a little strong fever.

sent_lc: Ti boug-la kouri koté lamezon-la, é li monté en lagalri.
sent_en: The little boy ran towards the house, he climbed onto the porch.

sent_lc: T'a petet,  ganñé ìn pé é petet ped ìn pé.
sent_en: You might win some, you might lose some.

sent_lc: Dìvan-la jeté mô garaj.
sent_en: The wind knocked over my garage.

sent_lc:
1. Ti garçon té shaken gìn trwa pyas
2. Mo té gìn trwa garçon é sìnk fiy. Mo çé ìn gro famiy.
sent_en:
1. The boys each had three dollars
2. I had three sons and five daughter. I've a big family

meaning:
1. to look at, watch
2. Look!
3. to seem like (look, appear, seem)
4. to concern
5. to keep
sent_lc:
1. Ga kommen li apé bafré.
2. Gar! Tou partou, gar!
3. Ça gad mal.
4. Ça gardé pa twa.
5. Gardé sécret çé ìn bétiz ki mo pa konné fé.
sent_en:
1. Look how he's stuffing himself.
2. Look! Everywhere, look.
3. That looks bad (that doesn't seem right)
4. It doesn't concern you.
5. To keep a secret is a sillyness I don't know how to do.

sent_lc: Ìn gar-manjé? Çé pou met tô lavesel, tô forshet é tô kiyær-yé, tô vær, ça tô bwa endan. Ça va dan lakwizin.
sent_en: A cupboard? It's for storing your dishes, your forks and spoons, your dishes, what you drink out of. It goes in the kitchen.

sent_lc: Garoshé yê moyær a vilain mérikan.
sent_en: Throw off their ways of being ugly Americans.

sent_lc: M'ashté ìn gato. Li té tou fé é li té bon.
sent_en: I bought a cake. It was ready-made and it was good.

sent_lc:
1. Ti-la té malad, mé li gayar astær.
2. Ça çé gaya ki chwé nom-la. Çé gaya-çala ki fé ça.
sent_en:
1. The child was sick, but he's healthy now.
2. That's the guy who killed the man. It's that man who did it.

sent_lc: Çé not gazolin pi dizeul pou met ça dan lê mashin.
sent_en: That is our gasoline and diesel to put in the engines.

sent_lc: Çé ìn gém Fronsé, gém pou lê Kadjin. Té pel ça bouré.
sent_en: It's a French game, a Cajun game, they called it Bourré.

meaning:
1. to heal, get better
2. to cure
sent_lc:
1. Mo té gen ìn bobo. Li geri byen.
2. Mo koné géri to bobo to gen apré tô janm avek lagres poul. To met souf endan lagres poul-la. Ça géri.
sent_en:
1. I had a sore. It healed well.
2. I know how to cure your wound you've got on your leg with chicken fat. You put sulphur in the chicken grease. It heals.

sent_lc: Ina pa gérison.
sent_en: There is no cure.

meaning:
1. to watch, look at
2. to watch, keep an eye on
3. to spy on
4. to watch out, be careful!
sent_lc:
1. Mo lìnm geté televizyon. Mo lìnm geté mô zanfan jwé laplot.
2. To gen piti en lagaldri. Mo gen pou geté li pa pou li tombé.
3. Nouzot t'apé geté yé dan krak.
4. Geté! Ina keken deryær to do é l'ap alé fou twa.
sent_en:
1. I like to watch TV. I like to watch my children play baseball.
2. You've got a child on the porch. I've got to watch him so he don't fall.
3. We would watch them through a crack.
4. Look out! There's someone behind your back and he's going to hit you.

sent_lc:
1. Prosh tou-le-jou nou gen diferan menyou.
2. Ki laj to gen?
sent_en:
1. Almost everyday we have a different menu.
2. How old are you.

meaning: to be allowed to, to have the right to
sent_lc: Vou konné, li di, "astær tou moun zegal, vou gen drwa pou voté paréy kom mwa."
sent_en: You know, he said, "now everyone is equal, you have the right to vote just like me."

meaning:
1. to have the chance to
2. to have luck, to be lucky
sent_lc:
1. Mo té si malad mo pa gen lashans pou ekri mô lord.
2. Mo té gen lashans. Blan kom nwa té okip apré mon.
sent_en:
1. I was so sick I didn't have the chance to write up my order.
2. I was lucky. WHites and blacks alike looked out for me.

sent_lc: Marshé dousman, ça gen lær li malad.
sent_en: She walked slowly, it seemed like she was sick.

sent_lc: La lê bon Françé ki sorti a la Frans vini resté isit. La yé yé vini lê gro moun-la é lê Kadjen té gen pœr!
sent_en: Then the real French, who came from France came to live here and they became wealthy and the Cajuns were afraid!

sent_lc: No té gen pou alé mé no shanjé nô nidé.
sent_en: We were going to go but we changed our minds.

sent_lc: L'apé di la verité. Li gen foutiman rézon.
sent_en: He's telling the truth. He's really right.

sent_lc: Mo t'apé atan é çé té mô tourn mé keken dot pasé dévan mò. Ina moun ke gen toupé!
sent_en: I was waiting and it was my turn but someone else went in front of me. Some people have nerve!

sent_lc: Inavé ìn benn la mìzik avek ìn kornen, gita é tanmbou.
sent_en: There was a music band with a tumpet, guitars and drums.

sent_lc: Ina ça laglas en shmen aswa. Yê fé fré. Çé ap alé fé laglas.
sent_en: There'll be ice on the road tonight. It's cold out. It's going to ice up.

sent_lc: Mo gen ìnta restan dan glasyé.
sent_en: I've got a lot of leftovers in the refrigerator.

sent_lc: To jish graté pou oté lalgli-la.
sent_en: You just skin it to take off the slime.

sent_lc: Ina moun ka di ça çé vô gogo, ina moun ka di ça çé vô deryær. Vo ka di ça çé mô chi, o kekishoj konm ça.
sent_en: There are people who call that your butt, there are people who say it's your behind. You can say it's my "ass" or something like that.

sent_lc:
1. Atan jishka to presk paré pou tourné tô gombo off avan to met tô shevré endan, parské to konné to va kwi yé va fé yé tro dìr.
2. kan ìn piti té gen ìn deklou, yé té pren ìn fler on ìn gombo. É fé ìn katalpa, met ça on la.
sent_en:

2. When a child had a boil they would take the flower from an okra plant and make a poultice to put on it.

meaning:
1. to go, to leave
2. to die
sent_lc:
1. Mo las. M'ap gonn kousé.
2. Vou konné vou gen chwé mò nou de gen gonn.
sent_en:
1. I'm tired. I'm going to bed.
2. You know you kill me we're both dead.

sent_lc:
1. Yé gen  pou oté mwa ìn ti kataral dan mô zyé gosh.
2. Kan Jean Sot rivé koté rwa-la yé prend ri a for Jean té parét gosh.
sent_en:
1. They have to remove a small cataract from my left eye.
2. When Foolish John arrived at the king's they started laughing because he looked so awkward.

sent_lc: Vou lwé lê gro bato, vou bezwen ìn gro zafær kom ça pou kouri laba dan Golf.
sent_en: You rent a big boat, you need a big thing like that to go out in the Gulf.

sent_lc: Té gen ìn gran jar, shak fwa mo sé kouri dan lamagazen-la mo sé pran mò ìn ti gout.
sent_en: She had a big jar, each time I would go in the store I would take a little drop.

sent_lc: La ça té gouté bon.
sent_en: That tasted good.

sent_lc:
1. Çé ìn fiy ki baset. Li pa gran.
2. Li gen ìn gran moustash.
3. Çé sô gran-gran-gran-piti.
sent_en:
1. She's a short girl. She's not tall.
2. He has a big mustache.
3.  They are his great-great-grandchildren.

sent_lc:
1. Piti-la grandi tro vit pou sô laj.
2.
sent_en:
1. The child is growing up too fast for his age.
2.

sent_lc: Mô gran-moman é mô gran-popa né isit.
sent_en: My grandmother and my grandfather were born here.

meaning:
1. old people
2. grandparents
3. famous person
4. adult

sent_lc: Mo kouri véyé mô gran-pær.
sent_en: I'm going to the wake for my grandpa.

sent_lc: Lontem pasé me sé jamé fermé mô lapot. Kité li gran-ouvær.
sent_en: A long time ago I never used to shut my door. I kept it wide open.

2. to scratch
3. to grate
4. to scale a fish
5. to skin
6. to strike a match

3. Vou ka graté li met dan tomato ketchup.

sent_en:

2. He's scratching himself because red ants bit him.
3. You can grate it and put it in ketchup.
4. I scaled my sunfish to take the scales off.
5. You just skin it to take off the slime.

meaning:
1. seed
2. pit (fruit)
3. grain, kernel (corn, rice, sugar)
4. testicles
sent_lc:

2. Ìn soko, li gen ìn ti lagrenn, li gen ìn lekosh en la.
3. Li koup lagrenn-la plì fen.
4. Ìn etalon, to pe pa gadé tou lê etalon. Ti wa çé grenn? In nonm vyen la, li koup avek ìn kouto, é pi oté sô grenn.
sent_en:

2. A muscadine grape, it has a seed inside and a shell outside.
3. He grinds the grains of sugar up finer.
4. A stallion, you can't keep every stalion. You see his testicles? A man comes along with a knife and cuts them off and removes his testicles.

sent_lc: Mo met mô morso lavyan dan mô lapwal é mo griyé li.
sent_en: I put my pieces of meat in my frying pan and I grill it.

meaning:
1. big, grand
2. tall, grand
3. important, grand
4. eldest

2. Yé té plì gro ke mò.
3. Lot boug-la ki té jwé réglé pou gro blan-yé.
4. Mô gro garson.
sent_en:
1. There's a big swamp on this plantation.
2. They were taller than me.
3. The other guy who played regularly for the important whites.
4. My oldest boy.

sent_lc: Tem-la brouyasé jodi. Li sìpozé pou kalm, mé ina ìn gro tem ke apé vini.
sent_en: The weather is drizzly today. it's supposed to be calm, but there's a big storm coming.

meaning:
1. to grow up
2. to grow
3. to get fat
4. to grow (raise)
sent_lc:
1. Mô mari, sô-chenn popa parlé meriken avek li kan li té vini op, kan li t'apé grosi.
2. To planté tô patat mé yé pa apé grosi.
3. T'ap grosi. T'ap vini tro gra.
4. Çé di pou grosi yé isit astær. Pa ka grosi depesh, yé vyen pa gro.
sent_en:
1. My husband, his own father spoke english to him when he was growing up.
2. You planted your potatoes but your not growing.
3. You're getting fat. You're getting too fat.
4. It's hard to grow them here now. You can't grow peaches, they don't get very big.

sent_lc: Alon alé grosri.
sent_en: Let's go to the grocery store.

meaning:
1. to move, move around
2. to rock, sway
3. to shake

2. Ìn vilen loragan tonbé en yé. Navir-la grouyé plen, mé li chombo ìn bon bout tem.
3. Lontem pasé yé té fé matla avek lapay. Yé gen ap pren ìn baton tou lê maten é soukouyé li byin. Otréman çé vini tou dìr si to çé pa grouyé li avek baton-la.
sent_en:
1. You're moving slowly. Why don't you move faster than that?
2. A nasty storm cam upon them. The ship rocked about, but it held on for a good while.
3. A long time ago they made mattresses out of straw. they had to take a stick every morning and shake it well. Otherwise it would get hard if you didn't shake it with the stick.

sent_lc: Mo tombé é fé mo lahansh mal.
sent_en: I fell and hurt my hip.

sent_lc: Ìn lamas, çá gen ìn lamansh kom ìn lahash.
sent_en: A sledgehammer has a handle like an axe.

meaning:
1. to yell, call out, shout
2. to chirp, sing
3. to bray
sent_lc:
1. Hélé apré piti-yé. Fé yé areté voyé kayou apré ìn-a-lot.
2. Ìn ki hélé la, mo pa koné ayou li yé, dan dibwa, kek plas.
3. Mìle-la hélé.
sent_en:
1. Yell at those kids. Make them stop throwing stones at each other.
2. There is one (mockingbird) singing, I don't know where he is, in the woods somewhere.
3. The mule is braying.

sent_lc: Çé ìn hont lamanyær yé fé piti-la.
sent_en: It's a shame the way they treated the child.

sent_lc: Hol-la plen moun.
sent_en: The dance hall was full of people.

sent_lc: L'apé asit en ìn bransh kom ìn ibou.
sent_en: He's sitting on a branch like an owl.

meaning: it is necessary to, to have to, must
sent_lc: To tro fronté avek tô moman. ifo pa to parl kom ça avek tô moman.
sent_en: You're too sassy with your mamma. You musn't talk like that to your momma.

sent_lc: Yé tord lavet-la, é yé shesh lasyet-la avek lavet-la ìmid.
sent_en: They wring out the dishrag and they dry the plate off with the dishrag damp.

sent_lc: Çé inposib pou mo fé ça t'olé jodi. Mo pa kapab.
sent_en: It's impossible for me to do what you want today. I can't.

sent_lc: Mo gin in frè dan vil, épi in lòt ki rès isi.
sent_en: I have one brother in the city, and another one who lives here.

sent_lc: Pètèt yé sé mèlé ça avèk in pe wiski.
sent_en: They might have mixed that with a little whiskey.

sent_lc: Tou di-long o bòr shmenn, inavé in ta zòdi.
sent_en: All along the edge of the road, there was a lot of trash.

meaning:
1. there is, there are
2. since, for, during
3. ago
sent_lc:

2. M'ap resté endan la Louzyon ina diz-an.
3. Fenm-la li mouri ina kat an, senk an.
sent_en:
1. There's bread, there's milk, there's a jar of jam at the bottom of the refrigerator.
2. I've lived in Louisiana for ten years.
3. That woman died four or five years ago.

sent_lc: Yé té si konten, inavé si lontem yé té pa kontré.
sent_en: They were so happy, they hadn't met up in such a long time.

sent_lc: Nom de Pær, é de Fis, é de Sent Espri, ensi-swat-il.
sent_en: In the name of the father, the son and the holy spirit, amen.

sent_lc: Li interesé dan mwa. L'ap fé zyé dou apré ma.
sent_en: He's interested in me. He's winking at me.

sent_lc: Mô moman, popa, tou té résté la isit, çé ça ki fé mo isi.
sent_en: My mom and my dad lived here, that's why I'm here.

sent_lc: Mo té gen ìn galenren isi, çé ìn zafær ki iti.
sent_en: I had a plane here, it's a thing that is useful.

sent_lc: Kan yé va chez mom, mom parl ça epi mô popa parl ça itou.
sent_en: When they go to mom's, mom speaks that (Creole) and my dad speaks that too.

sent_lc: To ja fé etoufé? Etoufé çé izé pou fé.
sent_en: Have you ever made an etoufee? Etoufee is easy to make.

sent_lc:
1. Si to met tô lamant tou-le-jou, t'apé izé li.
2. Ça çé donné yé in lashans pou izé yé nouvo langaj.
sent_en:
1. If you wear your watch every day, you're going to wear it out.
2. That would give them a chance to use their new language.

sent_lc: Ça té fé fresh, ça fé mo met mô jaket.
sent_en: It was cool out, so I put on my jacket.

meaning:
1. leg
2. lower half of leg (between knee and foot)
sent_lc:
1. Mô janm apé dormi. Mo gen ìn lakramp endan mô lajanm é çé lour.
2. Janm çé iska koté tô jnou.
sent_en:
1. My leg is asleep. I have a cramp in my leg and it's heavy.
2. The leg is up until your knee.

sent_lc: Kan to fé ìn jambalaya, to met tô zepi é tô touf ìn pe tô lavyann é to met diri. Çé kofær yé pel ça jambalaya. To gen tou ça malanjé ensanm.
sent_en: When you make a jambalaya, yuo  put your onion and garlic mixture and you smother your meat a bit and you put in the rice. That's why they call that jambalaya. You've go everything all mixed together.

sent_lc:
1. Ça çé ìn menti ça. Mo jamé di ça.
2. Senm kom si, dan le jen jan le kishoj apé kouri plì mal ke jamé.
3. Li jamé wa Lapen. Lapen laba apé dormi!.
sent_en:
1. That's a lie. I never said that.
2. It seems like it, for the young people things are going worse than ever.
3. She still didn;t see Rabbit. Rabbit was down there sleeping!

sent_lc: Prìmyé de janvyé to va swaté bonjour bon lanmen.
sent_en: On the first of January you go wish Happy New Year.

sent_lc: Ina ìn shyìn k'ap japé a onz-ær la é mo pa kapab dormi.
sent_en: There's a dog who's barking at eleven o'clock and I can't sleep.

sent_lc: Mo met ìn bonom fé a lapay endan mô jarden.
sent_en: I put a scarecrow made of straw in my garden.

sent_lc: Nou se planté le patat é le jardìnaj, é nou se manjé ça.
sent_en: We would plant potatoes and vegetables and we would eat that.

sent_lc: Ça rivé dan la smenn kat jedi.
sent_en: That will happen in the week of four Thursdays.

meaning:
1. frost
2. jelly
3. to shiver
4. to freeze
sent_lc:
1. Piti Bouki pa pi rivé koté dife paské inavé ìn gro jilé déyo.
2. Amaten mo manjé jilé é preno avek ìn ver dìlé.
3. Jour-la li té fé byin fret, tou moun yé t'apé jilé.
4. Si fre vini, li jilé li, li heg.
sent_en:
1. Bouki's child could not reach the fire because there was a big frost outside.
2. This morning I ate jelly and prunes with a glass of milk.
3. That day was very cold, everyone was shivering.
4. If the cold comes, it freezes, it gets sour.

meaning:
1. to throw
2. to throw away
3. to tear down
4. to spill, knock over
sent_lc:
1. Kan piti-yé manj, yé jèt manjé en planshè.
2. Mo prèsé papyé-la, é mo jèté li.
3. Divan-la jèté mô garaj. Mo gen pou rebati li.
4. Kò fé t'apé baskilé? To va fé li mal o to va jeté li.
sent_en:

2. I wadded up the paper and I threw it away.
3. The wind knocked over my garage. I had to rebuild it.
4. Why are you shoving him around? You're going to hurt him or you're going to knock him over.

sent_lc: Çé le kat de jìlyet. L'apé tiré ìn ta de péta.
sent_en: It's the Fourth of July. He's shooting a lot of firecrackers.

sent_lc: Mo va resté jishka la smen ki vyen.
sent_en: I'm going to stay until the week coming up.

sent_lc: Lontem pasé ìn pa la jistis. Yé té fé kom yé t'olé.
sent_en: Long ago there was no justice. They did what they wanted.

meaning:
1. just, fair
2. just, only, simply
3. to have just (recently done something)
sent_lc:
1. The man who runs the grocery store on Lake Road, he's fair with people.
2. We were seven brothers. There are only two of us left, myself and one in New Orleans.
3. My sister just left, she couldn;t stay because her husband is sick.

sent_lc: Mo t'ap fé li akrwa mo t'ap alé en in piknik.
sent_en: I was making him believe I was going on a picnic.

sent_lc: Nou patwa, çé té in langaj ki apé mèlanjé. Çé mèlanjé avèk, mo pens, le nwar, le zendyen, le françé. Ça tou mèlanjé. Epi avèk langlé osi.
sent_en: Our 'patois' is a mixed language. It is mixed with, I think, the language of Africans, Indians and French. And English also.

meaning: used to have (imperfect conugation of fé)
sent_lc: Kan yé vini yé fré chwé de trwa gro koshon.
sent_en: When they came they would have several big hogs killed.

meaning: did, used to do (imperfect conjugation of fé)
sent_lc: To min lòkoupasyon? Mo né isi, é nou fèzè travay on the farm.
sent_en: You mean my occupation? I was born here, and we did work on the farm.

sent_lc: De gro fwèt fèt avèk lapo bèf.
sent_en: Big whips made with cowhide.

sent_lc: Arèt ça. T'ap fé kònm in enbesil.
sent_en: Stop that. You're acting like an idiot.

sent_lc: Ça ina wrong avèk blan é nwa? Dèpi tem mo viv, çé té toujou konm ça, olé bat konm shyin é shat, mo pa fèt konmpren ça ina.
sent_en: What is wrong with whites and blacks? As long as I've been alive it's always been like that, they've wanted to fight like cats and dogs. I can't understand what's wrong.

meaning: to make it, succeed, get along
sent_lc: Komen t'ap fé li jodi-la? To pi malad?
sent_en: How are you doing today? You aren't sick anymore?

sent_lc: Fé gombo vert, mô vyé, çé jordi vendrédi, no va fé mèg.
sent_en: Make a vegetarian gombo, old pal. Today is Friday and we'll abstain from meat.

word: fé ni ènn ni de (v. phr.)
sent_lc: Balin fé ni ènn ni de, li pren la kord dan sô ladjoul é li du Kompair lapin, "Kan mo va tendé tambou mò halé."
sent_en: Whale did not hesitate, he told Brer Rabit, "When I hear the drum I will pull."

sent_lc: M'ashté in gato. Li té tou fé é li té bon.
sent_en: I bought a cake. It was ready-made and it was good.

meaning:
1. to take/bring some one back home
2. to remember, recall, bring back to mind
sent_lc:
1. M'a vini shèrshé twa é mènné twa bèk.
2. Kishoj d pasé lontem pasé, li kapab mènné li bèk.
sent_en:
1. I'll come and get you and take you back home.
2. Somthing that happened long ago, he can remember that.

meaning: to cheat on, run around on (one's spouse)

1. to sue, prosecute, take to court
2. to turn some one in to the police
sent_lc:
1. Yé pa ka sèrvi nòm-yé. Yé a sue twa, y'a mènné koté lalwa-la pou zafèr ça, vou pa ka sèrvi nom-yé.
2. M'ap mènné li a lalwa. Li chwé in nònm.
sent_en:
1. You can't use their names. They will sue you, take you to court, you can't use their names.
2. I'm turning him in to the police. He killed a man.

meaning: to remember, recall, bring back to mind
sent_lc: Mo gin pou jonglé in ti brin, mo té koné plin endan zafè-çayé byin, drwat la mo jish pa ka mènné yé dan latèt astèr-la.
sent_en: I have to think for a moment, I used to know a lot about that, I just can't think of them right now.

sent_lc: Yé té va pa lopital pou mènné bèbé.
sent_en: They didn't go to the hospital to have their babies.

sent_lc: Té janmé gin doktè avèk tou piti-yé mo mènné dan monn-la.
sent_en: I never had a doctor for any of the children I brought into this world.

sent_lc: To fé myè pou wa tô moman piti. Çé pou tô moman gin twa dan moun.
sent_en: You really should go see your mother, child. It's thanks to your mother that you're in the world.

meaning: middle row, space between two planted rows in a field
sent_lc: Ça ki apé dan milyè ran-la ramas dekann-la.
sent_en: The one who is in the middle of the row gathers the cane.

sent_lc: Milyè tô lamin.
sent_en: Your palm.

sent_lc: Mô mézon pli joli ke tô-kèn.
sent_en: My house is prettier than yours.

sent_lc:
1. Li travay lamòché in jou.
2. Inavé in boug koulèr, çé té in vyè lamòché pré.
sent_en:
1. He worked half the day.
2. There was a guy, he was black, but he was a half-breed.

meaning: by half (term used to describe share cropping in which one half of the harvest is paid in rent)
sent_lc: Mo koup sô o lamòché. Mo gin in bòs li dòn mo sô lamòché.
sent_en: I cut that in half. I have a boss who gives me half of it.

meaning: swear word, dirty word, bad word
sent_lc: Si yé tendé li çé jish i pè word koman ou jiré, tou zafè avèk vilen mònyèr-yé.
sent_en: If they heard it (Creole), it was just swear words and nasty things like that.

sent_lc: Li gin bon mònyèr.
sent_en: He has good manners.

sent_lc: To té gin sèré yé kèk mwòyèn si to té pa ka ashté yé.
sent_en: You had to store them somehow if you couldn't buy them.

meaning: any old way, no matter how

sent_lc: Mo apròn in bon mòrso.
sent_en: I've learned quite a lot.

sent_lc: É la si mo sé gin in ti mòrso d tèr ke mô popa té kité mwa.
sent_en: And if I had a little plot of ground that my dad had left me.

sent_lc: Ninpòr kalité t'olé jordi, t'alé gin li.
sent_en: No matter what kind you want today, you will have it.

sent_lc: Ninpòrt ça t'olé.
sent_en: Whatever you want.

meaning: any old way, no matter how
sent_lc: To iuvèr li avèk in ti swip, kiltivatè ou in shari, nimpòrt ki mònyèr.
sent_en: You open it up with a sweeper, a cultivator or a plow, it doesn't matter how.

sent_lc: Vyin nimpòrt ki tem.
sent_en: Come whenever.

meaning:
1. any old way, no matter how
2. as... as, no matter how
sent_lc:
1. Yé té pa ap kouri dan shmenn kouri nimpòr komen.
2. Nimpòrt komen maladi li yé, li tou-l-tem apé ri.
sent_en:
1. They weren't just running around the streets like that.
2. No matter how sick he is, he is always laughing.

meaning: woman of loose morals, slut, easy woman
sent_lc: É tou çé bouk dòrey, çé zafèr-çala, yé inavé pa d ça, non. Le moman té pa olé, paskè yé té krwa çé té de nimpòrt komen fiy.
sent_en: And all those earrings and those things, there wasn't any of that. Mothers didn't want it because they thought they were easy women (who wore that).

sent_lc: Mô granpèr sô nom té Alfons. Ça çé sô bon nom.
sent_en: My grandfather's name was Alphonse. That was his first name.

sent_lc: San Romèn çé sô dènyè nom.
sent_en: Saint Romain is his last name.

sent_lc: Amelis, li gin nom a mô popa é katrin, li gin nom a mô moman.
sent_en: Amelisse was named after my dad and Catherine was named after my mother.

meaning: to give someone one's last name (e.g. by adoption)
sent_lc: Li mèt en sô nom.
sent_en: He gave him his name.

meaning: first name, given name, baptismal name
sent_lc: Li di mwa, ""Mwa çé Sans-Quartyèr, Henri çé mô nom de baptèm."
sent_en: He told me, "I am Sans-Quartier, and Henry is my baptismal name."

sent_lc: Pa koné sô prèmyé nom.
sent_en: I don't know his first name.

sent_lc: Mo ti nom té Tout. Tou kèken pèl mwa Tout. Mô nom çé Fabiola.
sent_en: My nickname was Toute. Everyone called me Toute. My name is Fabiola.

sent_lc: Le nwa té pa ka kouri la, ni le blan non-pli.
sent_en: Blacks couldn't go there, neither could whites.

meaning:
1. famous man
2. big shot, important person
sent_lc:
1.
2. Li krwa li in gro nonm pou fé tou ça l'ap fé.
sent_en:
1.
2. He thinks he's a big shot for doing all he does.

sent_lc: Vyè nonm-la té gin in fizi. Li té va chwé trwa kat lapin kirey é tou kishoj.
sent_en: My old man had a rifle. He would go kill three or four rabbits, some squirrels, everything.

sent_lc: Ina in ta de miryaj dan syèl-la.
sent_en: There are a lot of clouds in the sky.

meaning: to get dark, be dark out
sent_lc: Lajourné apé vini kòrt. Y'apé fé nwar bonèr.
sent_en: The days are getting short. It's getting dark early.

sent_lc: Mo dèkoré mô dibwa Nwèl.
sent_en: I decorated my Christams tree.

meaning: to work in the yard, do yardwork
sent_lc: Byin, mo grandson mo gin la la, li vyin nètwayé mô lakou.
sent_en: My grandson comes over to help clean my yard.

sent_lc: Mwa mo di ina in ta endan mô-chèn nasyon ki gin mèm koulèr kom twa, é ina zòn endan tô-chèn bòr ki gin koulèr pa kòm mwa enkor.
sent_en: I say that in my race, there are a lot ofpeople who have the same skin color as you do, and there are some people on your side whose skin color isn't quite like mine yet.

sent_lc: Le koshon tou parti bòr é babòr.
sent_en: The pigs went every which way.

sent_lc: T'apé pasé la, t'apé kapoté la tèr bòr-la, ti vèv bòr-la, t'apé kapoté bòr-la, ti kontinué.
sent_en: You came along here, you turned the earth over to one side, you went along there, turned it over on that side, and continued on.

sent_lc: Ènn shari gin ènn orey. La, kan to rant li, le fé la tèr kapoté konm ça pou fé ton ran jish in en bòr.
sent_en: A plow has an "ear". So when you push it in, it turns the dirt over like that to make your rows even on one side.

sent_lc: Ina de, ènn shari é pi ina in flouk. Kan li rant dan la tèr li tir la tèr le de bòrr.
sent_en: There are two different ones, a single and a double plow. When you push it into the ground it throws dirt on both sides.

meaning:
1. the other side
2. on the other side (of any body of water, bayou, river, ocean,...)
sent_lc:
1. Misyè Pèryo rès en lòt bòr la.
2. Ina ènn ke rès lòt bòr. Li rès nòt koté flèv-la.
sent_en:
1. Mr. Periou lives on the other side of the street.
2. There is one who lives on the other side. She lives on the other side of the river.

sent_lc:
1. Mô garson, li bwa kafé. Li gin in tas o bòr li.
2. Mo mèt li o bòr navir.
sent_en:
1. My son, he drinks coffee, he's always got a cup with him.
2. I put him on board the ship.

sent_lc: En bòr isi New Orleans.
sent_en: On this side of New Orleans.

sent_lc: Plyé in vash, li ashté in koshon.
sent_en: Instead of a cow, he bought a pig.

meaning:
1. to take car of, watch, care for (an elderly person, a child, etc...)
2. to run, manage, tend (a store, business, etc...)
3. to pay attention to, listen to, heed
sent_lc:
1. Mo èlvé yé é yé, yé okip apré mwa.
2. Çé nòm-çala ki té okipé apré moulen-la.
3. To p'ap okipé pou ça m'ap di twa.
sent_en:
1. I raised them, and now they take care of me.
2. It was that man who took care of the mill.
3. You're not paying attention to what I'm telling you.

sent_lc: To vè pa kouté ça mo t'apé di twa.
sent_en: You don't want to listen to what I was telling you.

sent_lc: Ça ça vè di, çé ça çé diplon an. Diplon-la pli jèn, ça min li planté apré shiko li.
sent_en: What that means is that this is the cane that was planted for the first time. It is younger than the cane stubble, that means it was planted after that.

sent_lc: Mo pèl ça çé de mochi d françé. Mochi, ça vè min, yé sèy parlé françé mé yé pe pa.
sent_en: Well, I say they're half-French, half-French means they try to speak French but they can't.

sent_lc: Yé voulé pa nou parl kréyòl.
sent_en: They didn't want us to speak Creole.

sent_lc: Mo sé voudré gin in tas kafé.
sent_en: I would like to have a cup of coffee.

sent_lc:
Li vyin pa osi.
Li osi vyin pa.
sent_en: He is not coming either.

sent_lc: Tou nèg dan la mèsh, yé ça vini dan sô nid, otan lòn ke Tchoupoule rèsté lwin de li.
sent_en: All of the black folks in the swamp, they can coe to his nest, as long as Tchoupoule stays far from it.

meaning:
1. to dust
2. to give flavor to a dish
sent_lc:
1. Kwi, balyé, oté lapousyè, oté twòl zaryin, lavé.
2. Bout lavyann endan pou dòn li gou, oté lapousyè-la.
sent_en:
1. Cook, sweep, dust, remove cobwebs, and do the wash.
2. You add meat to it to give it taste, to remove the dust from it.

meaning:
1. to open
2. to cut open
3. to open, bloom (of a flower)
sent_lc:
1. Sô moman di li ouvri sô laboush pou li kapab senti ki ça li té manjé.
2. To sen le koshon, to grat li, to sen lim to shòd li avèk dolo sho. Apré ça to ouvèr li.
3. To ramas ça ki ouvri asé, ça ki ponjé asé.
sent_en:
1. His mother told him to open his mouth so he could smell what he was eating.
2. You bleed a pig and scrape it, you bleed it and scald it with hot water. Then you cut it open.
3. You gather the ones which are sufficiently opne.

meaning:
1. and
2. then, and then
3. with (very common for Point Coupée)
sent_lc:
1. Li tonmbé dòrmi, é mo té pa ka rèvéyé li.
2. Vou kité li èpi vou chaké li.
3. Mo rèté é yé jishka yé mouri.
sent_en:
1. He fell asleep, and I couldn't wake him.
2. You leave it and then you check it.
3. I lived with them until they died.

meaning:
1. to help
2. to help, prevent, keep from
sent_lc:
1. Mo té gin pou mèt in pilul enba mô lang pou èdé mô kèr.
2. To pa kapab èdé ça.
sent_en:
1. I used to have to put a pill under my tongue to help my heart.
2. You can't help that.

sent_lc: Mo té si malad mô pa in lashans pou ekri mô lòrd.
sent_en: I was so sick that I didn't have a chance to write up my order.

meaning:
1. spice
2. combination of onions, gralic, sweet pepper and parsley
sent_lc:
1. Kan mo té fé la bisk, dabòr momèt tou mô zépisdan larou.
2. Kan y'apé fé in frikasé, tô toufé zépi-yé é to mé tô lavyann.
sent_en:
1. When I made bisque, first I put all my spices in the roux.
2. When you make a fricassee, you smother your onions and garlic mixture and you put in your meat.

sent_lc: Li froté sô né avèk sô de pat, pi li grouyé sô zorey, li tèrné é pi li asi.
sent_en: He rubbed his nose with both paws, then he shook his ears, he sneezed, and then he sat down.

sent_lc: Li étidyé asé pou gin in profèsyon.
sent_en: He studied enough to have a profession.

meaning:
1. to amaze, astonish
2. to be surprised
sent_lc:
1. Ça té si tèlman étòné yé ke yé rèté pou tendé é gèté.
2. Li té étòné wa li rèsté dir si lontem.
sent_en:
1. That surprised them so much that they stopped to listen and watch.
2. He was surprised to see it remain for so long.

meaning:
1. étouffée
2. to stew, smother (cooking term for cooking in a covered pot)
3. to suffocate, smother, choke
sent_lc:
1. Mo té gin in ta de lakè krèbis. Mo fé in étoufé d krèbis.
2. To nètwayé ça épi to toufé ça.
3. Bébé-la çé toufé.
sent_en:
1. I had a lot of crawfish tails. I made crawfish etouffee.
2. You clean it and then smother it.
3. The baby is choking.

meaning:
1. where
2. from where
3. where, wherever
4. (from where)
sent_lc:
1. Mo pa koné éyou ça yé astèr-la.
2. Aou to sòr?
3. Yé té konyé li dan la sèrvèl pou fou li partèr eyou yé pe fonn so lagòrj.
4. Li fé pli frèt dan bout-çala. Éyou to sòti çé pli frèt.
sent_en:
1. I don't know where it is now.
2. Where are you from?
3. They hit him in the head to knock him to the ground, where they could cut his throat.
4. It's colder there. Where you come from it's colder.

sent_lc: Yé voyé in bomb-la dan navir é ça èksplozé endan navir-la.
sent_en: They threw the bomb onto the ship and it exploded in the ship.

sent_lc: Kan to tir,  la prèshyon de èksplozyon dan la kartoush, l'apé sòti.
sent_en: When you shoot, the pressure of the explosion in the cartridge comes out.

meaning:
1. to raise (a child)
2. to raise (animals)
3. to grow, raise (a crop)
4. to rise, get up, come out
5. to breed
sent_lc:
1. Mo té né é èlvé isi a Sesilya.
2. Li pa in dezwa dou, li èlvé dan in dezè ou dan in flèv ki lwin isi.
3. Yé elvé tou zafè-çalayé, ina diblé, lavwenn, tou ça yé èlèv ça dan la nòr.
4. Li té koné di fifolé-yé té koné èlvé dan le swa-la.
5. N'a manjé le lapin jish la ivèr. Dòné yé la shans pou yé èlvé.
sent_en:
1. I was born and raised here in Cecilia.
2. It's not a tame goose, it's raised in a field or on a river that's far away from here.
3. They raised all those things, there's wheat, oats, all that, they raised it in the North.
4. He used to say that will-o-the-wisps came out at night.
5. We would only eat rabbits in the winter, to give them a chance to breed.

sent_lc: Li yin èlvé é li polit.
sent_en: He's well mannered and he's polite.

sent_lc: Mo èlvé en la vyann.
sent_en: I was raised on meat.

meaning:
1. to one another, to each other
2. one another
sent_lc:
1. Ça toujou apé batayé méça fé pa ènn-a-lòt ditòr.
2. Piti-yé t'apé kashé pou pa ènn-a-lòt trouvé yé. Yé t'apé jwé kashé-fwèt.
sent_en:
1. They're always fighting but they don't hurt each other.
2. The children were hiding so they wouldn't find each other. they were playing hide and seek.

meaning: interrogative marker placed at the beginning of a sentence, for yes or no questions (did...?, do...?)
sent_lc: Èské to alé a legliz bon matin?
sent_en: Did you go to church this morning?

sent_lc: Èskizé mwa. Mo marshé en tô pyé.
sent_en: Excuse me. I stepped on your foot.

sent_lc:
1. Vou èspèré jishka li vini jonni.
2. Gin gro lofis ayou yé spè pou doktè.
sent_en:
1. You have to wait until it turns yellow.
2. There is a big office where they wait for the doctor.

meaning: none, not any, not a single one
sent_lc: Mô lòt sèr-yé, yé pa koné aryin. Pa ènn dan yé, jish mwa.
sent_en: My other sisters, they don't know any (Creole). Not one of them, just me.

sent_lc: To pa mèm komansé travayé.
sent_en: You haven't even started working.

sent_lc: Nou pa ni Pa Kleman ni Tant Sharlòt.
sent_en: We have seen neither Pa Clement nor Aunt Charlotte.

sent_lc: Yé té pa gin sèlman in lonp pou limyè yê mézon.
sent_en: They didn't even have a lamp to light their house.

sent_lc: Li pa jish shant, li pen osi.
sent_en: Not only does he sing, he paints too.

sent_lc: Si yé pa fé li astèr la yé pe é fé li, yé pe gin moun pou fé li pli ta.
sent_en: If they don't make one now (a dictionary of Louisiana Creole), they won't make one later, because there won't be any speakers around later.

sent_lc: Mé nô vyè paran a nouzòt té èsklav.
sent_en: But our ancestors were slaves.

sent_lc: Li pa paran avèk pèrsòn dòt. Li pa paran avèk Ledif ni aryin konm ça.
sent_en: He's not related to anyone else. He's not related to the LeDuff's or anyone like that.

sent_lc: Li di, "O non, non, mo krwa pa m'alé jwé" li di, en parlon ça li di, "trap banjo-la ki laba dòn kwen!"
sent_en: He said,"Oh no, I don't think I'm going to play." But as he said that, he added, "grab the banjo over there in the corner."

meaning: to speak "thick", speak with an accent
sent_lc: Yé parl gra.
sent_en: They speak thick.

meaning:
1. to say a prayer over some one or something
2. to talk about
sent_lc:
1. Si to koupé tô janm o tô bra é disan é señè, to pa ka rèté li, l'a jish parlé en li fé in lapryè.
2. Nou gin pou aprenn komen sé pou parlé kréyòl nou-mènm, pasé ça arèt tou zòt parlé a nouzòt.
sent_en:
1. If you cut your leg or your arm, and it's bleeding and won't stop, he just says a prayer over it, and that will stop it.
2. We have to learn how to speak Creole ourselves, because that will stop all of you from talking about us.

sent_lc: Li mandé li ki çé ça li t'apé parlé pou.
sent_en: He asked him what he was talking about.

sent_lc: Mo parlé sègré avèk li.
sent_en: I whispered to her.

sent_lc: Yé gin tou yê parlaj.
sent_en: They all have their way of speaking.

sent_lc: Spirit-la, li té kontini viv é li té koné fé tou moun pè, çé paskè pou ça yé jamé trouvé tou larjan-la.
sent_en: The spirit remained alive and he scared everyone, and that is why they never found the money.

sent_lc: Mè nouzòt nou travay a in parti o kar, mé nou té lwé.
sent_en: But we worked partly as sharecroppers, yet we were renting the land.

sent_lc: Li parti dèryè sô lapip.
sent_en: He went to go get his pipe.

sent_lc: Parskè parti a Rout jishka Pon Bro, kan mo té jenn, Pon Bro çé stil blacktop.
sent_en: Because from Ruth to Breaux Bridge, it was still a blacktop road.

sent_lc: Kan kou-d-tem ap vini, gwalan-yé ap votijé tou partou.
sent_en: When bad weather is coming, the sea gulls fly around everywhere.

sent_lc: La vou pou mèt vou liv paré.
sent_en: Then you can get your book ready.

sent_lc: Yé vyin paré.
sent_en: They get ready.

sent_lc: To té bwa wiski, çé té parey kòm in bon medikaman.
sent_en: If you drank whiskey it was like a good medicine.

meaning: it's all the same, it doesn't matter

1. among, between
2. between
3. to enter, go in.
4. to bring in
sent_lc:
1. Yé té parl mérikèn entré yé.
2. Byin, mo fé mô lapat dipen é tou mo pren mô lavyann, é la mo met li ent é la mo bake li.
3. Mo kouri koté katolik, pèr-la ke mo vini pou entré, di mwa to pa ka asi enho.
4.
sent_en:
1. They spoke English amongst themselves.
2. Well, I make my bread dough and I take my meat, and then I put it between and I bake it.
3. I went to the Catholic (church), and when I went to go in the priest told me "you can't sit there."
4.

sent_lc: Çé in gro shyin. Li va adrèsé laparol.
sent_en: He's a bigshot who's going to deliver a speech.

meaning: swear word, dirty word, bad word
sent_lc: Li t'apé sèrvi tou kalité parol sal.
sent_en: She was using all kinds of bad words.

sent_lc: A byin, Ròklò pren paròl, demaré mwa é lèsé mwa shapé.
sent_en: Well, Roclos said, "untie me and let me go."

sent_lc: Kan li sou, li sèrvi tou kalité vilen paròl.
sent_en: When he's drunk, he uses all kinds of bad words.

sent_lc: Ina in mo pou ça mé mo rapèl pa.
sent_en: There is a word for that but I don't remember.

sent_lc: Li p'olé dòn sô piti tèté, li fé in lòt fenm dòn sô piti tèté.
sent_en: She doesn't want to breast-feed her baby, she has another woman breast-feed him.

sent_lc: Çé vré, ça pa gin in ta larjan mé li gin lalibèrté, li gin konfyans é, sakré tònèr, le nèg té kontan!
sent_en: It's true, hedidn't have a lot of money, but he had freedom, he had trust, and good heavens, the black man was happy!

sent_lc: Tònèr mékraz! Boss a parti galopé konm çala, cé té in assassin.
sent_en: Thunder strike me! Boss took off running like this was an assassin.

meaning: to turn one's back (on some one)
sent_lc: Ina moun yé mari é viv, yé tourné yê do.
sent_en: There are people whose husband is alive and they tuirn their backs on them.

sent_lc: Yé té priyé en kréyòl. O si vou té tanndé ça! Mé si mo té ka tourné mô-kènn lalanng kònm ça, mo té vini proud.
sent_en: They used to pray in Creole. Oh, if you could have heard that! But if I was eloquent like that I would be proud.

sent_lc: Ça tourné yê latèt é yé bwa pli enkòr jishka yé té sou mouri.
sent_en: That made their heads spin and they drank some more until they were dead drunk.

sent_lc: Mo va gardé televizyon. Mo va tourné li ann.
sent_en: I'm going to watch television. I'm going to turn it on.

sent_lc: Tourné stov-la òf.
sent_en: Turn the stove off.

sent_lc: Yê zyé té tourné lanvè, kan yé té shanté.
sent_en: Their eyes rolled back when they were singing.
